summary: The session focused on the impact of the COVID-19 pandemic on the Tampa Bay Buccaneers' interactions with fans and the adaptations made to maintain engagement. Key points included the initial absence of fans at games, the implementation of virtual experiences, and the gradual return of fans under safety protocols.
facts: The Tampa Bay Buccaneers initially played games without fans in attendance during the COVID-19 pandemic to ensure safety. The team implemented social distancing, face coverings, and temperature checks for fans attending games. The number of fans allowed at Buccaneers games was capped at 25% of the stadium's capacity during the 2020 season. The Buccaneers engaged fans through virtual pre-game and post-game activities, including contests and Q&A sessions with players. The team launched a 'Bucs Squad' initiative to provide fans with different levels of engagement, including virtual meet-and-greets with players. The Buccaneers have been monitoring the COVID-19 situation and working with local health officials to determine safe fan attendance. The team has continued to prioritize safety while adapting to the pandemic by providing alternative options for fan engagement.
